features.title: Where you sleep ‚Äî a per-night strip showing which nights are booked and which are gaps
features.what: The Bookings view now opens with a üõè coverage strip ‚Äî one cell per night of the trip (the final departure day is excluded since it needs no bed). Each night is colour-coded: a green cell with the hotel/stay name when a booking spans that night (check-in ‚â§ night < checkout, so checkout day doesn't double-count), or a dashed terracotta gap when nothing covers it. The header counts covered nights at a glance (e.g. ‚Äú6/7 nights covered‚Äù). A campervan/motorhome counts as the bed it is, matching the ‚ÄúStill to book‚Äù logic so the two never disagree. Tapping an uncovered night jumps straight to the manual add form pre-filled as a hotel for that date (check-in 15:00, checkout next morning) so you can lock the stay in one tap. On the seeded Alpine trip every booked night shows covered with its stay name and any uncovered night shows a clear, tappable gap.
features.verify_note: Independent agent confirmed merge 568f4b2 on origin/main (accommodationStrip in js/core.js, stripHtml + data-booknight quick-add in js/bookings.js, .bk-night/.bk-strip in css/app.css). node tools/test-core.mjs exits 0 with 9 new accommodationStrip guards ok (per-night cells, departure-day excluded, checkout-exclusive coverage, campervan-as-bed, uncovered‚Üífalse, empty edge cases). Live served checks 200 with markers Travel Companion + accommodationStrip + bk-strip.

features.title: Booking timeline ‚Äî every reservation in one chronological, day-by-day view
features.what: A new ‚è± Timeline tab gives every booking for the trip its own dedicated, read-only chronological view: grouped by day and sorted by start time, each row shows the type icon, the time (start‚Äìend), provider, confirmation # and price. Time conflicts are surfaced two ways ‚Äî a header banner counts how many bookings overlap, and any clashing entry is highlighted inline with an ‚Äúoverlap‚Äù tag ‚Äî so a double-booked window jumps out at a glance. It reuses the same overlap detection as the Bookings warnings, so flags stay consistent across the app. On the seeded Alpine data the reservations render in time order under each day with no false conflicts; a deliberately overlapping pair flags both entries.
features.verify_note: Independent agent confirmed merge 3041b71 on origin/main (bookingTimeline in js/core.js, new js/timeline.js, timeline view registered in js/app.js, precached in sw.js). node tools/test-core.mjs exits 0 with 6 new bookingTimeline guards (date-sort, within-day time-sort, overlap flagging, empty/undated edge cases). A throwaway import fed two overlapping flights ‚Üí both overlap:true. Local + live served checks 200 with marker 'Booking timeline'.

features.title: ‚ÄúStill to book‚Äù ‚Äî auto-spots the nights and travel legs you haven't booked
features.what: The Bookings view now shows a üß≥ ‚ÄúStill to book‚Äù checklist that pressure-tests your reservations against your itinerary. It flags two kinds of gap: any night with no accommodation booked, and any move where your overnight base changes (the day's ‚Äúsleep‚Äù location differs) but no transport covers the hop. It's smart about road trips ‚Äî a campervan/motorhome counts as the bed while you've got it, and any vehicle you're holding covers every driving leg, so a van trip on a single rental booking raises nothing. Gaps are grouped by date so you can see at a glance what's left to lock in. On the seeded pre-exchange trip it correctly lists the un-booked Genova‚ÜíFirenze and Firenze‚ÜíBologna legs while showing zero false alarms on the fully-booked hotel nights; the Alpine campervan loop shows clean.
features.verify_note: Independent agent confirmed merge f06ba5c on origin/main (coverageGaps in js/core.js, stillToBookHtml in js/bookings.js, .bk-tobook in css/app.css). node tools/test-core.mjs exits 0 with 6 new coverageGaps guards ok. On real seeded data: pre-exchange flags exactly the missing transport legs with 0 false lodging gaps; the Alpine trip-spanning campervan suppresses all ~15 base changes (only the genuine post-return tail remains). Live served checks 200 with markers coverageGaps + stillToBookHtml.

features.title: Add a booking by hand ‚Äî provider, end time, location & travellers
features.what: The Bookings view's ‚ÄúÔºã Add a booking manually‚Äù form now captures a full reservation, not just the basics. Alongside type, title, start time, confirmation # and price, you can now add the provider (e.g. Booking.com), an optional end time, the location, and the travellers on the booking (comma-separated). Whatever you enter is trimmed, auto-filed to the right trip by its start date, and saved into your local bookings overlay's manual list ‚Äî so a hand-added hotel shows up in the timeline on its own day exactly like a booking imported from Gmail, and survives a reload. Empty optional fields are dropped cleanly so cards stay tidy, and a typed location with no map pin now shows as a üìç line on the card.
features.verify_note: Independent agent confirmed merge cb5e914 on origin/main: new pure buildManualBooking() in js/core.js trims fields, files via assignTrip, splits pax on commas, defaults currency, stamps source:'manual'; js/bookings.js form adds provider/end/location/pax inputs and the submit handler routes through buildManualBooking into the manual[] overlay (ctx.save). node tools/test-core.mjs exits 0 with 4 new buildManualBooking guards ok (full hotel‚Üífiled to alpine, bare‚Üínulls, blank/defaulted price). LIVE served checks 200 with marker buildManualBooking in js/bookings.js and js/core.js (no propagation lag).

features.title: Suggest a packing list from the weather + your plan
features.what: The Checklists view has a new ‚Äúüéí Suggest packing list‚Äù button that builds a packing checklist tailored to your actual trip ‚Äî no guessing, no generic template. It reads each day's weather forecast (the same open-meteo data the Today view uses, cached so it still works offline) and scans your day plans, then assembles a deduped list: rain shell + waterproof shoes if any day shows rain, a warm fleece/down layer for cold alpine lows, hat & gloves and thermal base layer when it's near freezing, sun hat & sunscreen for hot or sunny days, plus activity-aware extras ‚Äî hiking boots & a daypack if your plan mentions a funicular, summit or mountain trail, and swimwear & a quick-dry towel if there's a lake or swim. Base travel essentials (passport, charger, power bank, meds, water bottle, walking shoes) are always included, with laundry sheets for trips of five days or more. One tap drops it in as a normal checklist you can tick off.
features.verify_note: Independent agent confirmed merge 64516b2 on origin/main: pure suggestPacking(dayInfos) in js/core.js derives items from per-day weather + plan text, 'üéí Suggest packing list' button in js/checklists.js gathers weather via weather.js and adds a 'Packing (suggested)' checklist. node tools/test-core.mjs exits 0 with all 10 new suggestPacking guards ok (rain‚Üíshell, alpine lows‚Üíwarm layer, funicular‚Üíhiking boots, lake‚Üíswimwear, base essentials always, no dups). Local + LIVE served checks 200 with marker 'Suggest packing list' in js/checklists.js (live propagated on retry).

features.title: Today view shows where you are in the day
features.what: On the day of travel, the Today view no longer shows a flat list where every stop looks the same. Each timed stop is now marked by where you are in the day: the stop you're in right now is highlighted with a gold border and a NOW pill, your next stop carries a NEXT pill, and stops you've already finished fade out with a strike-through. A stop with only a start time counts as ‚Äònow‚Äô until the next one begins. At a glance ‚Äî without reading every time ‚Äî you can see what's happening now and what's coming up. This only kicks in on the real current day; previewing a future or past day shows the plain plan.
features.verify_note: Independent agent confirmed merge dc13fc2 on origin/main: pure planProgress(plan, nowHHMM) in js/core.js (now/next/past/'' per stop), today.js imports it and renders NOW/NEXT pills + highlight gated on rel==='today', new B11 guards in tools/test-core.mjs. node tools/test-core.mjs exits 0 (7 planProgress assertions ok); agent's own independent logic assertions passed. Local + LIVE served checks 200 with markers (core.js 'function planProgress', today.js 't-pill-now'); live propagated on first attempt.

features.title: Print / share ‚Äî day-by-day itinerary handout
features.what: A new ‚Äúüñ® Print day-by-day‚Äù button at the top of the Itinerary view turns your trip into a clean paper backup. It opens your browser's print/save dialog with a dedicated print stylesheet: all the interactive chrome ‚Äî the trip switcher, view tabs, add-a-place box, suggestion chips, drag handles, detail drawers and action buttons ‚Äî drops away, and each day breaks onto its own page. What stays is exactly what you'd want on a printed handout or saved PDF: the day number and date, the day's places in order with their times and notes, the route summary, and that day's bookings. Print to paper or ‚ÄúSave as PDF‚Äù for an offline backup that works with zero signal.
features.verify_note: Independent agent confirmed merge b5f243e on origin/main: @media print block in css/app.css (hides #viewtabs/.addplace/.suggs/.grab/.dayactions via display:none, forces per-day .daycard+.daycard break-before:page, keeps .planlist/.bkchip/.dayhead) and window.print() wired to the #itprint button in js/itinerary.js. node tools/test-core.mjs exits 0. Local + LIVE served checks of css/app.css (marker '@media print') and js/itinerary.js (marker 'window.print') all HTTP 200; live already propagated.

features.title: Forward-to address label in bookings
features.what: The bookings view now shows a clear, dedicated line ‚Äî ‚Äúüì® Forward booking confirmations to wchongyu2001@gmail.com‚Äù ‚Äî with a one-tap Copy button, so you always know exactly where to forward a flight/hotel/train confirmation. Forwarded mail flows in through the existing daily pipeline sync and lands in this timeline automatically; no app setup needed. The address lives in a single INBOUND_ADDR constant, ready to swap to a dedicated inbound-parse address later.

features.title: Faster expense entry + budget vs actual per day
features.what: Logging a spend is now near-instant: the quick-add form needs only an amount ‚Äî the note is optional and category, who-paid, and 50/50-vs-payer-only split all default sensibly, so it's type-the-number-and-Add (‚â§3 taps). Underneath, the settle-up summary still spells out exactly who owes whom. And a new "Budget vs actual" card breaks the trip down day by day ‚Äî each day's guide estimate next to what you've actually logged, with the difference coloured (terra = over, pine = under) and a running total at the top ‚Äî so you can see at a glance which Alpine days ran hot or came in under.

Live core.js & budget.js served HTTP 200 with the budgetVsActual marker on first try (no Pages lag).

features.title: Nearby discovery into itinerary
features.what: Open any place in the itinerary and a new "Nearby eat & do" panel now suggests real spots around it ‚Äî restaurants, caf√©s, bars, gelaterias, viewpoints, museums and attractions ‚Äî pulled live from OpenStreetMap (free, no API key, cached for offline). They're ranked nearest-first, and one tap on a chip drops it straight into that day's plan with its category as a note. So when you're standing in an Alpine town wondering where to eat or what's worth a look, the options are one tap away instead of a separate map search.
features.verify_note: Independent agent confirmed merge b19bf3d on origin/main (pure overpassUrl/parseOverpass/nearbyCacheKey in core.js; .pd-nearby drawer + data-near chips pushing via setPlan in itinerary.js); test-core exits 0 with 7 new B07 guards (named-only, deduped, nearest-first, amenity‚Üífood/tourism‚Üíact mapping); live core.js & itinerary.js serve HTTP 200 with the parseOverpass and pd-nearby markers after Pages propagation.

features.title: "Can I make it?" timing warnings
features.what: Your itinerary now sanity-checks the clock. Between consecutive stops, the travel-time connector compares the real driving/walking time against the gap you've actually scheduled ‚Äî if the hop won't fit, it turns red and warns "‚öÝ tight by N min" so you know before you're standing there late. Whole days get checked too: if your stops plus travel blow past a sensible waking budget, a "‚öÝ Packed day" banner appears. On the seeded Alpine trip, the zero-buffer Sirmione ‚Üí Jamaica Beach hop flags as tight while the relaxed afternoon stops stay quiet.
features.verify_note: Independent agent confirmed merge f0bff42 on origin/main (pure legGapMins/legFeasibility/dayLoad in core.js, leg-tight + packed-day banner in itinerary.js); test-core exits 0 with new B06 guards; an independent import check confirmed a tight leg (90m travel vs 30m gap) ‚Üí {tight:true} and a comfortable leg ‚Üí {tight:false}; live core.js & itinerary.js serve HTTP 200 with the feature markers.

features.title: Unassigned-booking triage
features.what: No booking can silently fall through the cracks anymore. The üì• Unassigned inbox in the bookings view now catches every booking that belongs to no trip ‚Äî ones with no trip set, ones tagged 'unassigned', and ones still pointing at a trip you've since removed. Each gets a clear "‚Üí assign to‚Ä¶" dropdown; pick a trip and it's filed instantly and remembered (saved to your bookings overlay).
features.verify_note: Independent agent confirmed merge bb862dc on origin/main (new pure orphanBookings in core.js, wired into bookings.js inbox); test-core exits 0 with 2 new B05 guards (flags unassigned/no-trip/unknown-trip, clean booking ‚Üí none); live js/core.js serves HTTP 200 with the orphanBookings marker.

features.title: Booking gap & conflict detector
features.what: The bookings view now scans your trip and flags problems in a dismissible banner: bookings dated outside the trip, two bookings overlapping the same time window, and broken flight chains (you arrive at one airport but the next flight departs another ‚Äî a missing connecting/return leg). Conservative by design ‚Äî hotels and campervans never count as time clashes and clean multi-city itineraries stay silent, so the seeded Alpine & Iceland trips raise nothing.
features.verify_note: Independent agent confirmed merge 66f686f on origin/main (bookingWarnings in core.js, bk-warnings banner in bookings.js); test-core exits 0 with 6 new B04 guards; detector raises 0 warnings on real preexchange/alpine/iceland data and 1 on a deliberately broken booking; live core.js serves 200 with the marker.

features.title: Mobile "Today" view
features.what: A single, thumb-friendly screen for the road: today's date and weather, the day's ordered plan with times, and the next upcoming booking. It auto-opens when the trip's dates contain today, and is always reachable from the new Today tab (previewing day 1 before the trip starts).
features.verify_note: Independent agent confirmed: js/today.js + 'today' view registration + auto-select on origin/main, sw.js precaches it; test-core exits 0 with new pickTodayDay/nextBooking guards; live js/today.js serves HTTP 200 with the Today-view marker.

features.title: Offline map tiles + last-known weather
features.what: Map tiles you've already viewed are kept in a dedicated cache (surviving app updates) and re-served with no signal ‚Äî blank gaps instead of broken images where uncached. The day forecast is persisted per location, so weather still renders offline from the last-known copy.
features.verify_note: Independent agent confirmed: tc-tiles tile cache + OSM handling + weatherCacheKey present on origin/main; test-core exits 0 with new B02 guards; live sw.js serves HTTP 200 with the tc-tiles marker.

features.title: Offline PWA shell
features.what: Service worker + manifest cache the whole app shell and every trip's JSON, so the Alpine itinerary, map and Today view open with no signal.
features.verify_note: Independent agent confirmed live: homepage, sw.js and manifest all serve HTTP 200; service worker registered; test-core green with new precache guards.
